name: Maggie Hassan
subject: something to tell you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Good morning --
I sat down with my campaign team recently, and I have to tell you that our conversation has me worried. No matter how you look at it, we're in for an extremely tough fight.
If there's one takeaway, it's that there is no room to slip with our fundraising -- even now. **This seat is very competitive, and our fundraising will determine whether we can set the record straight when corporate special interests mislead voters about our values.**
We came up a bit short in March, so we've got to raise $20,000 before mid- April to make up ground. Look, you're one of my strongest supporters so I have to ask: [**Please give $3 or as much as you can to help us build a winning campaign. Anything you can give makes a difference, and I would be so grateful for the help.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc299/2a380d31/3b0e8b8c/f11633/2791095568/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX2dlbmVyYWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMjAxODA0MTNfbWlkbW9udGgyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_general?refcode=e220180413_midmonth2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")
As a Senator, I've made it a priority to stand up to the worst parts of President Trump's agenda. I'm working to not just defend the progress we've already made, but to continue to push us forward.
Corporate special interests don't like that very much, and you can bet that they're putting a huge target on my back -- especially since we only won our 2016 race by 1,017 votes. They're going to spare no expense to defeat me, everyone, and we have to be prepared to fight back.
That's why raising $20,000 before April 15 is so important. Please chip in $3 or whatever amount works for you so that we can protect this Senate seat:** [https://hassan.bsd.net/Mid-April-Deadline](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc299/2a380d31/3b0e8b8c/f11633/2791095568/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX2dlbmVyYWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMjAxODA0MTNfbWlkbW9udGgyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_general?refcode=e220180413_midmonth2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")**
I can't win without you, so thank you so much for your help.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan
> [Contribute](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc299/2a380d31/3b0e8b8c/f11633/2791095568/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX2dlbmVyYWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMjAxODA0MTNfbWlkbW9udGgyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_general?refcode=e220180413_midmonth2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")

name: Robert Foster
subject: Week 17 in the books
body:
From the campaign trail
---
| | | | | | |
---
|
---
|
---
| |
---
| Dear friend,
Week 17 of the campaign is in the books. And as promised, I want to keep you informed from the campaign trail.
We started this campaign in my hometown of Hernando, and we have been campaigning hard ever since, and we won't be slowing down anytime soon.
This week, we started in my home county speaking to community and business leaders at the Olive Branch Rotary, and we ended the later part of the week in northeast Mississippi with a room full of conservatives who are fired up about our "political outsider" campaign to be Mississippi's next governor.
---
|
---
| This week I also had the opportunity to sit down for an interview with The Oxford Eagle about our campaign and what we are running on. If yo haven't seen the article, you can read it [here](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001gsom90OsPs6RGyOTI7VnpaYhRfAo4ruK5r5uDTnK7axXYQWnfxh5wcDC0tv3HiikamvrVntolOH7TMhR3A5S1YAFbsbEd-G1DpguwQNIJTb8FrDbpYYcrDlP7ZgeECgwImtBS9r6_Gs7vYq7P_mVQKynF8JXktp-YACX_Y96malendnpI5kKgFYLyC7bkKth9PPUIGJXBmNeXzGcCvm7iVNutTWaTr-544EBLX8FfgFb59EI2eKC2VDFYetYKJLzrOBwHDtQ8hcn-6zUkc7SiA==&c=W9mnnB0A3IN-5uZGwN4n1CN8knYZSzDKnrXSK0iP0Zb3ZiGIW6O8iA==&ch=ppipNW5OxTQp0yCBrlER46kut38U18CRqdSmqVI6-1nhZhe-IWckzg==)[.](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001gsom90OsPs6RGyOTI7VnpaYhRfAo4ruK5r5uDTnK7axXYQWnfxh5wcDC0tv3HiikamvrVntolOH7TMhR3A5S1YAFbsbEd-G1DpguwQNIJTb8FrDbpYYcrDlP7ZgeECgwImtBS9r6_Gs7vYq7P_mVQKynF8JXktp-YACX_Y96malendnpI5kKgFYLyC7bkKth9PPUIGJXBmNeXzGcCvm7iVNutTWaTr-544EBLX8FfgFb59EI2eKC2VDFYetYKJLzrOBwHDtQ8hcn-6zUkc7SiA==&c=W9mnnB0A3IN-5uZGwN4n1CN8knYZSzDKnrXSK0iP0Zb3ZiGIW6O8iA==&ch=ppipNW5OxTQp0yCBrlER46kut38U18CRqdSmqVI6-1nhZhe-IWckzg==)
In 2015, many of you hired me as state representative for District 28, something I don't take lightly. As many of you know, session started this past week for us at the Capitol. Being here for three years, I have learned people are hungry for a change to the status quo. And as a political outsider, I am excited about bringing a fresh perspective and outside-the-box thinking to the office of governor.
We have had a busy and productive past 17 weeks, but I am asking for your continued support as we build on this momentum. Mississippi can-and will-be an even better place to live, work, and call home.
Heather and I are beyond grateful for the tremendous support and warm welcome you have shown us since we started this campaign - my biggest challenge yet. Heather and I covet your prayers and hope you will stick with us as we work towards a successful primary in August.
Thank you and God Bless!
---
|
---
| Robert Foster

subject: quick thing for this a.m.
body:

We're building this campaign centered around you--our supporters. Your priorities and your concerns about the Trump administration are really important to us.
**Can you tell us a bit about what's on your mind re: defeating Trump in 2020? name: Stacey Abrams
subject: Our nation's promise
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Action
body:
The battle for voting rights is at the core of every fight for our most basic civil rights, Friend. |
---
[](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa69f/34a064cc/7f4b63c/70386b2/119748996/VEsH/ "https://www.fairfightgeorgia.com/")

| | | Friend - My belief in the responsibility to fight voter suppression started when I was growing up. My parents were both civil rights activists as teenagers in Mississippi. My dad was arrested signing people up to vote when he was 16. My mom used to take my five siblings and me as children with her to vote on Election Day. We were raised to understand that voting is directly tied to the services and policies that you want to see in your community. Yet, voter suppression acts as a means to deny people of the services they deserve from our government and the reality of policies that would better their communities. The battle for voting rights is at the core of every fight for our most basic civil rights. [**Will you make a contribution today to fight back against voter suppression and help expand opportunity for all?**](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa69f/34a064cc/7f4b63c/70386b3/119748996/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?refcode=IE041319b") In 1965, our government made a promise to the people with the Voting Rights Act. We promised to defend the right to vote for every person and strive towards a more inclusive democracy. But unfortunately that promise has been gutted. We must restore that commitment by dismantling a system of suppression that undermines the will of the people. I believe that we can fix what is broken, and we can make right what is wrong. I know that we can demand a fair fight for our nation by ensuring that every eligible voter can access the ballot. I will continue to fight for every person, whether or not they share my beliefs and my background, because we share a common responsibility to our country. **[Will you join me in demanding a fair fight for every eligible voter by making a contribution today? Together, we will lead the way forward on voting rights and restore our democracy.](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa69f/34a064cc/7f4b63c/70386b3/119748996/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?refcode=IE041319b")** Onward,
Stacey

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: It's time: abolish the Electoral College
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2b3da/6cd27893/4a662c75/1581fc68/1289904778/VEsH/ "http://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | The person who wins the most votes should win an election. Period. This is especially critical for our nation's commander-in-chief. _No p_ resident, Democratic or Republican, should _ever _be sworn into office after losing a popular vote. The electoral college is badly outdated and allows this to happen. We saw it most recently in 2016, when Donald Trump lost by more than 3 million votes... but still "won" the presidency. It's time for the electoral college to go. That's why I've introduced a constitutional amendment to abolish it once and for all. **[Sign the petition: tell Congress to pass my constitutional amendment to abolish the electoral college.](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2b3da/6cd27893/4a662c75/1581fc69/1289904778/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/ "https://www.jeffmerkley.com/petition/electoral-college/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-04-electoral-college-email-2")** _Jeff_ [Sign the Petition](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2b3da/6cd27893/4a662c75/1581fc69/1289904778/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/ "https://www.jeffmerkley.com/petition/electoral-college/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-04-electoral-college-email-2") |

subject: Happy Tax Day
body:
"Tax Day is always bad because Americans are overtaxed. But this Tax Day, we can celebrate the fact that an overwhelming majority of people..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fc3f7c8a1a&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [Happy Tax Day](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=043ac74250&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Washington Examiner_
“Tax Day is always bad because Americans are overtaxed. But this Tax Day, we can celebrate the fact that an overwhelming majority of people are being taxed less, thanks to the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, which Republicans passed in late 2017,” the _Washington Examiner_ editorial board writes. “No fewer than four out of every five taxpayers saw a reduction thanks to the law.”

**🎬**** Watch: [Ivanka Trump on the newly doubled Child Tax Credit](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=42ab0b5a80&e=3ee4985eea)**
---
|
---
| [From ‘No Crisis’ to ‘Breaking Point’: Mainstream Media Changes its Tune](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9475780a6f&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“So let’s stick to one big, basic truth: There is no crisis at the southern border,” _The Washington Post_ editorial board proclaimed after President Donald J. Trump’s State of the Union address in February. After U.S. officials apprehended or turned away more than 100,000 illegal immigrants crossing our southwest border last month, “the same media outlets are now scrambling to accurately portray the border crisis.”

**🎬**** Former Obama DHS Secretary: [“This is a crisis by any measure.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=281dc723b7&e=3ee4985eea)**
---
|
---
| [The Fraying Edges of Universal Health Care](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9eb19c526a&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Washington Times_
“If you’re wondering what Democrats have in mind when they tout 'Medicare For All,' look no further than England. There are more reports of the U.K.’s National Health System’s collapse, this time featuring horror stories of rationing care for the elderly,” Tammy Bruce writes. Government-run healthcare “is the ultimate in population control.”
---
|
---
| [US Weekly Jobless Claims Drop to the Lowest Level Since 1969](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8965cc7538&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Reuters_
Weekly jobless claims dropped to a near 50-year low last week, pointing to America’s continued market strength, _Reuters_ reports. “Initial claims for state unemployment benefits fell 8,000 to a seasonally adjusted 196,000 for the week ended April 6, the lowest level since early October 1969. Claims have now declined for four straight weeks.”
---
|
---
| [Pence Confronts Venezuelan Ambassador at UN: ‘You Shouldn’t Be Here’](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6e39fc5604&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“Vice President Pence called out Venezuela's United Nations ambassador to his face while addressing the body's Security Council on Wednesday -- a speech he used to declare it's time for Venezuelan dictator Nicolas Maduro to step down,” Adam Shaw reports. The Vice President said it is time for the United Nations “to stand with the people of Venezuela as they march for freedom.”

**🎬****[Watch: Vice President Pence calls out Maduro’s representative](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d3d234c40d&e=3ee4985eea)**
---
|
---
| [The Worst Federal Student-Loan Program](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=387b8eb69c&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Wall Street Journal_
“College debt feels out of control. Outstanding student loan-liabilities, almost all from federal loans, have more than doubled since 2009, to $1.57 trillion,” Neal McCluskey and Diego Zuluaga of the Cato Institute write. “Recognizing the ever-increasing burden of student loans, the Trump administration has proposed limiting the amount of money available through PLUS loans . . . That may have the rare feature of being both a step in the right direction and an area of bipartisan agreement.”
---
|
---
| [A Serious Blow to Iran’s Terror Specialists](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=07944ce69d&e=3ee4985eea)
-_New York Post_
“In a bold step, President Trump has formally designated Iran’s Revolutionary Guard a terrorist organization — a move that carries serious financial implications for Tehran and lengthy prison terms for those who provide material support,” the _New York Post_ editorial board writes. These sanctions are “vital to addressing the key elements that were deliberately omitted from the Obama nuke deal: Iran’s missile program and its fomenting of global terror.”
---
|
---
| [Trump Signs Executive Orders to Fast-Track Oil and Gas Pipelines](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5c225a1544&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Daily Caller_
“President Donald Trump signed a pair of executive orders aimed at speeding up oil and gas pipeline permitting, including limiting the ability of activists and states to block key energy projects,” Michael Bastasch reports. “President [Barack] Obama lamented the lack of shovel-ready jobs; President Trump is going straight to the operating engineers who will build our infrastructure to show how he will clean out the stables and make America stronger,” said Dan Kish, senior fellow at the Institute for Energy Research.
---
| | |

---

name: ElizabethWarren.com
subject: How to change Washington
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Ending corruption | | |
---
Elizabeth always says that in Washington, money doesn't just talk - it _screams_.

Over and over again, the rich and powerful get their way - passing tax breaks for billionaires and giant corporations, slashing regulations on Wall Street, and rigging the system so no one else has a fighting chance at success.

But it doesn't have to be that way. There are a lot more of us than there are of them - and in a democracy, that matters.

Elizabeth has spent her career fighting to level the playing field for everyone. She doesn't take money from Washington lobbyists or corporate PACs. She's fought back against big Wall Street banks and powerful interests, protected consumers, gotten results for working families in the Senate, and laid out the most ambitious plan to end corruption in Washington since Watergate.

And now, Elizabeth is running for President. We're building a grassroots movement to change the way Washington works - to get government working for working people.

**Strong, volunteer-driven, grassroots organizing is the only way to elect Democrats up and down the ballot who will fight back against corruption, from local government and state houses to Congress and the White House.**

**We've got to get organized - now - to win back the White House and elect Democrats up and down the ballot.

[By signing up to volunteer, you'll get plugged in with other people near you who are ready to fight for what you believe in. You'll meet new people, learn new skills, and get ready to make a difference. Sign up here.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f3c7/45c30650/b33f0a9f/6694be54/584634481/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiJ9/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/web-volunteer?source=ws-nd-ns-non-em2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=")**

Thanks for fighting alongside us,

Team Warren

name: Kristen Orthman
subject: Elizabeth sets a fast pace (just ask TMZ)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Every day on the trail brings something different, like house parties with neighbors getting together in living rooms, organizing events in packed theaters or school gyms, even seeing how maple syrup | | |
---
I go around the country with Elizabeth as her communications director.

She sets a fast pace on the campaign trail -- sometimes literally (you might have seen me placing third in the background of that TMZ clip of her running to catch a train.)

It's because she really prioritizes going to as many places -- and talking with as many people -- as possible. Here's some of what that's looked like, by the numbers:
* Holding **48** events (with more on the books for today and tomorrow in New Hampshire)
* In **12** states plus Puerto Rico
* Taking over **225** unfiltered questions from voters
* More than **22,000** attendees seeing her in person, and about **13,000** people staying afterwards to get selfies and chat with her
* Not to mention, the access she's given the media: she's answered hundreds of questions from the press in 39 press conferences and close to 100 interviews

**Now, here 's the thing: Elizabeth is only able to do this because she's forgoing private fundraisers with wealthy donors and hours and hours of calling for big checks.**

**[Our campaign is only possible because more than 135,000 grassroots donors have chipped in. So here's one more number for you: Can you make your first contribution to help keep Elizabeth on the road and keep her voice front and center in this race?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f3d8/45c30650/b31af746/6694bde4/2811063881/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWFwcjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNDEzZW0xLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-apr19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190413em1-full")**

Every day on the trail brings something different, like house parties with neighbors getting together in living rooms, organizing events in packed theaters or school gyms, even seeing how maple syrup gets made in New Hampshire.

And next week, we're headed to South Carolina, Colorado, and Utah.

She hears directly from people about what's going on in their lives -- dealing with student debt, fighting to be able to get health care, trying to find good affordable child care, getting a check back from Citibank thanks to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau.

She wouldn't hear all those same types of stories at swanky fundraisers where people have to drop thousands of dollars to get in the door. And she wouldn't get to spend as much time building our grassroots movement.

**So we 're running a different kind of presidential campaign -- only powered by people like you giving what they can when they can -- with no support from federal lobbyists, corporate PACs, or PACs of any kind.**

**[If you want to see a strong grassroots campaign show how we can get big money out of politics-- and if you like what you see when Elizabeth's out on the road talking about her big ideas -- chip in $3 or whatever you can afford now:](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f3d8/45c30650/b31af746/6694bde4/2811063881/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWFwcjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNDEzZW0xLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-apr19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190413em1-full")**

Thanks,

Kristen

body:
[1]Beto for America

In just a moment, I’m going to ask you to make a donation to Beto’s
campaign. But first I wanted to send along a rough outline of some of our
campaign’s most important strategic priorities. Here’s how I see it:

1. Beto is working his heart out to earn votes.

* Beto is on the road, talking to people and generating local media
coverage.
* 83 campaign stops so far (more than any other candidate).
* 476 questions from voters taken.

2. We’re activating our grassroots voter contact machine.

* Hundreds of volunteer events are already on the map.
* Thousands of Beto supporters are knocking on doors.
* We’re using scientifically proven tactics like canvassing to earn
votes.

3. People-powered fundraising.

* All people, no PACs. Funding this campaign with emails like this
one.
* Donations help us hire organizers state-by-state and eventually
to run advertisements to introduce Beto to voters who don’t know
him yet.

It’s too early to put stock in the polls, but we are in a good position up
near the top of a large field. Every dollar donated -- especially at this
early stage -- helps Beto gain upward momentum in this race. Can you make
your first donation

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17403?t=1001&akid=27362%2E444211%2E903Dsr ]https://act.betoorourke.com/go/beto-surge

Beto will win this race if and only if all of us come together to build a

Jen O’Malley Dillon
Campaign Manager
Beto for America

name: Cory Booker
subject: Together, we will win. Together, we will rise.
body:
| | |
---
| A few moments ago, I laid out my vision for this country at our biggest campaign event to date. First of all: Thank you, friend -- for your support as we seek justice, not just for some, but for all. **Today we are impatient for that justice.** And our sense of urgency, our impatience, comes from the most demanding of values -- it comes from love. Love of our families. Love of our communities. Love of country. Love for each other. And to many people across the country who don't speak English, I want to say: **Yo voy a ser un presidente para toda nuestra gente.** (I know this is a long message, but please keep reading -- this isn't like your typical emails.) 56 years ago, a young preacher was arrested on Good Friday while demonstrating against segregation, **and on this very day, Saturday, April 13, 1963, he was waking up in a jail cell in Birmingham.** The same jail cell where he wrote on the margins of a newspaper that had been smuggled in to him these words: _" For years now I have heard the word "Wait!"_ _This "Wait" has almost always meant "Never."_ _We must come to see...that 'justice too long delayed is justice denied.'"_ He spoke to the possibility of what we can achieve when we **realize our own power** and **refuse to wait**. That young preacher was Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Generations of Americans have shown us what was possible when they refused to wait. Now it's our turn. America, we know our history -- it is a perpetual testimony to impatient, demanding, unrelenting people who in every generation brought a bolder, more real, more American justice. But we still have work to do. America -- we can't wait. America -- we will not wait. **Join me, and together, we will fulfill our pledge to be a nation of opportunity for all. A nation of liberty and justice for all.** I am so thankful for your support -- your dedication has kept this grassroots team motivated and inspired. [_**Will you consider making your first donation to our campaign? Even $1 will take this campaign to the next level.**_](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-apr-em?refcode=em_fr_20190413_CB_20190413-fr-CB-postrally1-actives_2020ndnr_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") We can't wait when powerful forces are turning prejudice into policy and rolling back the rights that generations of **Americans fought for and heroes died for**. We can't wait when this administration is throwing children fleeing violence into cages, banning Muslims from entering the nation founded on religious liberty, and preventing brave transgender Americans from serving the country they love. We can't wait because we have a criminal justice system so deeply baked with bias that it ruthlessly discriminates against Black people, Brown people, low-income people, and people with mental illness and addiction. We can't wait when we have an economy where people who work two or three jobs -- even pick up extra shifts -- still can't pay their bills. We won't wait for criminal justice reform. We will end the system of mass incarceration in America. We won't wait to legalize marijuana at the federal level. We will pass universal background checks, ban assault weapons, and close the loopholes that allow people who should never have a gun to get one. We will bring a fight to the NRA like they've never seen before. And we will win. We won't wait to meet the crisis of climate change. We won't wait for real, inclusive economic justice. We will protect workers' rights to organize and empower unions. We will build an opportunity economy where there are good-paying jobs and fair wages in every neighborhood and where the dignity of work is respected. We won't wait to deliver a great education to every child. We won't wait to fix our broken health care system. We won't wait to fix our broken immigration system, because immigration is a source of strength. We won't wait to stop the dangerous assault on women's rights. We will demand reproductive justice, because women must have the freedom to make decisions about their own bodies and economic futures. And as President, I will appoint Supreme Court justices who will defend _Roe v. Wade_. We won't wait any longer for equal justice under the law. Because "Justice for all" has to mean justice for everyone. Together, we will run at the tough challenges. **Together, we will again do the things that people tell us are impossible.** Together, we will win. And together, we will rise. Let's start today. Cory

This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc761b/5fca0d19/1bcdd5b1/291ab0da/2920049667/VEsP/ "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc761b/5fca0d19/1bcdd5b1/291ab0d9/2920049667/VEsHBQ/ "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc761b/5fca0d19/1bcdd5b1/291ab0d8/2920049667/VEsHBA/ "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. We believe these emails are an important way to stay in touch, but if at any point you'd like to stop getting these updates, you can unsubscribe [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc761b/5fca0d19/1bcdd5b1/291a8f27/2920049667/VEsHBw/ "https://act.corybooker.com/unsubscribe").
Is this not your preferred email address? You can update your contact info [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc761b/5fca0d19/1bcdd5b1/291ab0db/2920049667/VEsHBg/ "https://corybooker.com/join?source=em_emfooter").

| | Nobody likes tax season. And this year has brought an unwelcome surprise for many taxpayers -- their refunds got smaller. All that talk Trump does about cutting taxes hasn't made things better for that many Americans after all. Maybe we shouldn't be that surprised by another empty promise from Washington, but we need more from our elected leaders. [_**I want to hear from you all: what do you think about your refund this year? Have the tax cuts impacted you in other ways? Share your thoughts with me here.**_](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa289/18d6a294/4ae0c9cc/26d6ac71/1649777793/VEsE/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190413") | | | [SHARE YOUR STORY >>](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa289/18d6a294/4ae0c9cc/26d6ac71/1649777793/VEsF/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190413")
---
A recent survey showed that many Americans plan on refund checks to help cover big purchases and pay down debt. I believe we need true tax reform that helps lower the burden on middle-class families and small businesses instead of debt-financed giveaways for billionaires and the well-connected.
[_**I will be a Senator who listens to all Tennessee families --that's why it's important for me to hear from you.**_](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa289/18d6a294/4ae0c9cc/26d6ac71/1649777793/VEsC/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190413")

---
Thank you,
James

name: Carolyn Booker
subject: My son Cory
body:
| | |
---
| Friend, There's a joke my son Cory likes to tell about how behind every accomplished child is an astonished parent. And I can truly say that I am indeed astonished, thrilled, and exceedingly proud of Cory today. He just wrapped up his first big campaign event in Newark, and I can't stop smiling. I've watched Cory dedicate his entire life to public service -- fighting for his community, fighting for his state, and fighting alongside Americans across the country who want better lives for themselves and their families. And I know that he will be the kind of president who won't run away from tough problems. Cory's leadership will bring our country together in the face of difficult challenges, and his character will make us all proud -- of him and our country. But he and I both know that he can't make change alone. **Cory 's team tells me that the most important thing people can do to help right now is make a donation to build this campaign and ensure his spot in the national debate -- [_so will you chip in to my son 's campaign today?_](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-apr-em?refcode=em_fr_20190413_CAB_20190413-fr-CAB-postrally2-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** I've had the honor of witnessing and participating in many historic moments in my life -- from attending Fisk University during the civil rights era with Diane Nash and John Lewis to participating in sit-ins protesting segregation in Charlottesville and Nashville in the 1960s and helping plan the March on Washington in 1963 where I heard Dr. King share his dream with the world. Today, I am so honored to play a small part in this historic moment, too. [_**I hope you 'll join me.**_](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-apr-em?refcode=em_fr_20190413_CAB_20190413-fr-CAB-postrally2-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Thank you so much, Carolyn Booker
[](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-apr-em?refcode=em_fr_20190413_CAB_20190413-fr-CAB-postrally2-actives_actives_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")
